entries.title: Diverging
entries.note: I really wish things were easier. I would love to continue straight with you, but I know what that asks for, and I can't give it.
entries.lyrics:
[verse]
We’ve both hurt each other badly before
But the hill we face this time is something more
No wall to push through
No hedge to climb
The fate of our future held in a change of mind

[verse]
For 16 years we’ve found a way around
Found always a helping hand when we hit ground
And the hand is there still
It always will
But now it pulls in two directions

[chorus]
There is love in those eyes, love that remains
Love enough to wet the brush and paint over the pains
But there’s hurt there too, hurt that remains
From not knowing where our path leads
Accommodating the new unwanted me

Is this love enough
When the road seems tenderly diverging?

[verse]
We trusted our ground so we stopped watching our step
Now the earth has shifted and there's damage left
Took the certainty we’d built
Replaced it with guilt
And left behind shreds of a map we’d drawn together

[bridge]
I don’t know, I don’t know, I don’t know, please stop asking
I can’t offer the thing you need, I’ve lost it myself
All I can say is that maybe it’s time to put down the gloves
And stop fighting our problems

If all we have together is 3 months
Before the shape of our love takes a turn
Can we use our hands to hug and hold
To have and to harbor
Reflect and revel
On what we still have?

[chorus]
There is love in those eyes, love that remains
Love enough to fill all gaps and paint over the pains
But there’s hurt there too, hurt that remains
From not knowing where our path leads
Accommodating the new unwanted me

[outro]
Is this love enough
When the road seems tenderly diverging?

entries.title: Quiet Hours
entries.body:
Dear Nicole,

It's six in the morning and the kettle is doing its small operatic thing on the stove. I have been awake for an hour because I wanted to be the one to hear the day first today, which is a strange ambition but feels like the right one.

You are still asleep in the next room, and from here I can see the bend of your shoulder and the part of the duvet you always claim is yours. You are wrong about that, of course. The duvet is communal. I will keep losing this argument graciously.

Three years. I keep wanting to find some new way to say it but the only honest version is the boring one — *it has gone fast and I am grateful*. That's it. That's the whole sentence.

I made coffee. It is too strong. You will complain. I will pretend to be sorry.

Happy anniversary.

m.

entries.title: She Should Remain
entries.note: You knew the song I made for you on the train was the first. But little did you know it wouldn't be the last.
entries.lyrics:
[verse]
It’s 3 AM and she’s finishing off a bullet
It’s 3 AM and she’s downed a gin and tonic
The night has just begun,
but not necessarily for fun
Either way she’ll take her time and get it done

[verse]
It’s 5 AM and she’s built a new component
It’s 5 AM and round 2 kicks off in a moment
The night is still in an early stage
Her fuel is a mix of party spirit and rage
Either way she’ll survive long enough to get it done

[chorus]
Her vibe isn’t something announced but unwrapped
Like light that pulses through a door’s half-open gap
Like sound seeping from a wall half-broken
The night ends before she does
The curtains hesitate because
Unless you’re brave, she should remain unwoken

[verse]
It’s 8 AM and she’s just sent off a slide deck
It’s 8 AM and everyone else is a fucking wreck
The night is done but she doesn’t slow
She missed that memo dozens of years ago
She is surviving long enough to get it done

[verse]
It’s 10 AM and she’s shutting off her iMac
It’s 10 AM and the host is sleeping in the back
It’s time for coffee fill the fucking cup
Her grab driver has just woken up
She has survived just long enough to get it done

[chorus]
Her vibe isn’t something announced but unwrapped
Like light that pulses through a door’s half-open gap
Like sound seeping from a wall half-broken
The night ends before she does
The curtains hesitate because
Unless you’re brave…
she should remain unwoken

[bridge]
Man what the fuck is up with her
She lives her life right on the edge
I can’t keep up but I want to watch
Watch her balancing on that beautiful edge
Not for the fall
Nor the tension of the close call
But for the moment when life lets her step off that path
And lets that ethic be guided by her creative half

[instrumental]

On that day
I’ll stay awake

[chorus]
Her vibe isn’t something announced but unwrapped
Like light that pulses through a door’s half-open gap
Like sound seeping from a wall half-broken
The night ends before she does
Morning breaks but she doesn’t not
Unless you’re brave…
she should remain unwoken

entries.title: Not a Valentine
entries.note: Probably I won't send this one.
entries.body:
There is a thing I have not said to you, and the reason I have not said it is that I think you already know, and saying it would feel like reaching for the light switch in a room that's already lit.

But just in case.

I think you are the most attentive person I have ever met. You notice the temperature of a room as soon as you enter it. You notice when I have been pretending to listen to a podcast and you notice when I am actually listening to one. You notice the way the woman at the corner shop is having a bad week, and you remember her name, and you ask after the dog.

I used to think attention was a small thing. It is not a small thing. It might be the largest thing.

If you are reading this, then I gave it to you after all, which means I was braver than I thought I would be, and you should know that you are the reason for that.

Not a valentine.

— m.

entries.title: Second Year
entries.note: Written from the kitchen, two years in.
entries.body: Two years. The flat smells like the bread you started yesterday. I have read the same paragraph four times and I keep losing it to the sound of you humming. I think that's the whole report. I think that's the year.

entries.title: The Day After
entries.body: I forgot to say it yesterday so I am saying it today: the best part of the anniversary was the bit afterwards, where nothing was special, and we just made dinner. That's the actual measurement. I think we are doing well.

entries.title: First Anniversary
entries.note: One year. Still figuring out the format.
entries.body: A year ago today we were eating cold noodles on the floor because the table hadn't arrived. I keep thinking about that, the way the boxes looked, the way you laughed. We have a table now. The laugh is the same. That's the part I wanted on record.

entries.title: A Half Year
entries.note: Not quite an anniversary. Counting anyway.
entries.body: Six months in and I am keeping a small list of the things you do that I did not expect to love. Top of the list this week: the way you read the back of the cereal box out loud, in a voice meant for theatre.

entries.title: Three Months
entries.body: Three months. I keep waiting for the part where I get tired of how you laugh in your sleep. The part has not arrived. I am beginning to suspect it isn't on the schedule. I will keep you posted.

entries.title: One Month
entries.body: One month is a silly thing to mark and I am marking it anyway. You left a mug in the sink with a tea bag still in it and I felt nothing but tenderness about it, which is, I think, the diagnosis.

entries.title: The Thing About Tuesdays
entries.note: Recorded after the fact — close to verbatim.
entries.body:
What I will miss about my kitty.

Hey meow meow,

So, I expect this will be a bit of a sobering experience, but a good one. It's true that maybe I don't spend enough time thinking about the good things that we have going. But I do, and I realize what an amazing relationship we have, even if it goes bad once in a while. So as you requested, here are a few of the things that I treasure most about our relationship, and what I will most definitely miss about you if we ever break up.

-Never a dull moment: I've never been bored with you, ever. It may not always be good news, but there's always news with you. I haven't lived the most exciting social life since I graduated from Lasalle, and in a way, I kind of live socially through you. You have that kind of social life that I never had, and in a way I get to experience what it's like being friends with the kind of people that I might not want to get close to. But now that I am closer to them, it's quite an experience, and has allowed me to change how I see things and people. I'm super grateful for that, and I don't know that I'll be able to have it again if we don't make it, so I will miss that.
-Loyalty: You're really the most loyal person I know. You've made your share of mistakes, and even did some dumb things after getting with me (like showing John your underwear at Zirca), but I never doubted that your intent was anything close to disloyal. I'm not saying that I don't believe you've ever had doubts, especially when you were feeling down, but through it all, I know that you would never, ever cheat on me, and I'm glad that I was able to find someone who feels just as strongly as me about this core issue.
-Honesty: You are so crazy honest (with me) that it sometimes comes across as a bad thing. Now don't get me wrong, I am also incredibly honest, but sometimes the way you approach things without sugarcoating them at all can be a bit abrasive. On the one hand, it can be tiresome and troubling, yes, but on the other... I know that I can trust you, and that you'll tell me everything that I want to hear, and everything that I don't, but need to. I know a lot of people, myself included, that would rather keep certain things to themselves just to avoid the trouble of having to deal with them, but you're not one of those people. I can really appreciate that. I'm not saying that it's the best approach however... a little balance can be good- but I know that if it's something that matters, even if it puts you in a bad light, you'll tell me.
-Our kittyness: Obviously I'm going to miss everything that makes us kitties. I'm not stupid, and I know that it is sometimes just a shield that we put up (or at least, that I put up) to help me cope with things, to give myself a bit more time. It's easy not to talk about serious issues when the only thing that's happening in coversation is noms and cuddles and wuggles. But the kittyness is -not- just a shield. It's really a huge part of who were are together, part of our shared identity, and I love every last bit of it with every piece of my heart. I will never, ever forget our kittyness, what it means to us, and how much each huggle that we had means to me.
-Your face (and body): I've said this a million times, and alluded to this issue in probably every single serious letter I've written to you, and probably will continue to do so in the future- I find you attractive (sexy attractive, not just cute attractive), and I will miss your looks if we ever break up. You are doubtlessly the most attractive girlfriend that I've ever had, and I will miss that.
-Your artistic ability: I'm really super impressed with what you can do, aesthetically. You touch photoshop, it's gold. You touch paper clay for nearly the first time, you create an amazing looking wand. I have no doubt that anything you put your mind to doing artistically, you will do amazingly. One of the reasons that I push you to do more graphic design work is because I know that you can do truly great things there, and that I just want to see more of your work. A lot of times when I'm walking around and see something artistic, I think to myself: "Nicole could do better if she wanted to try".
-Your dedication to me: I DON'T take it for granted. I really appreciate just how much you care for me, and I'm impressed by the number of chances that you give me on issues in which I clearly fall short, again and again.
